mnemonics: Align & Conquer: Series and DataFrames align on their index (row labels) and columns before any operation, just like lining up soldiers by name before giving orders.
keyPoints: Broadcasting (vectorization) applies an operation to all elements, saving you from writing slow loops.
A DataFrame is a dict-like container of Series objects. Operating between them follows the same alignment rules.
Missing values (NaN) will result from any operation where indexes don't align, signifying 'no data' for that row/column pair.
commonMistakes: Assuming index order instead of checking alignment, leading to silent misoperations.
Misunderstanding the axis parameter (axis=0 for rows, axis=1 for columns) in methods like .sum() or .drop().
